title: Local session transcript retention window
why_it_matters: Claude Code stores local session transcripts in plaintext under ~/.claude/projects/ and, at startup, deletes session files and other application data older than cleanupPeriodDays (documented default 30 days, documented minimum 1 — the settings reference states that setting 0 fails with a validation error, and documents no maximum). A larger configured value keeps plaintext session transcripts, including code and prompts, on local disk for longer. This rule observes only the value present in the inspected user-level settings.json; it does not determine Anthropic's separate server-side data-retention period (documented as 30 days standard for commercial users and consumer users who opt out of training, 5 years for consumer users who opt in, or Zero Data Retention for qualifying Enterprise configurations), which depends on account type and plan and is never inferred here.

outcomes.when: cleanupPeriodDays is explicitly set to 30 days or fewer
outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config keeps the local session transcript retention window at or under Claude Code's documented 30-day default.

outcomes.when: cleanupPeriodDays is explicitly set to more than 30 days
outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config extends the local session transcript retention window beyond Claude Code's documented 30-day default, keeping plaintext session transcripts under ~/.claude/projects/ on local disk for longer.

remediation.summary: Restore the local session transcript retention window to the documented 30-day default or shorter.

outcomes.when: cleanupPeriodDays is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective local session transcript retention window cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: cleanupPeriodDays is unset in the user-level config; Claude Code's documented 30-day default applies absent an override, but uninspected managed, project, local, or CLI-flag layers may supply a different effective value.

outcomes.when: cleanupPeriodDays is set to a value outside the supported integer range
outcomes.message: cleanupPeriodDays is set to a value outside the supported integer range — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: cleanupPeriodDays is set to a value outside the supported integer range — raw values are never displayed

sources.title: Claude Code settings
sources.notes: Documents cleanupPeriodDays in the settings.json schema table: default 30 days, minimum 1, 'Claude Code deletes session files and other application data older than this period at startup... Setting 0 fails with a validation error,' with no documented maximum. Also documents the five settings scopes (managed, command line, local, project, user) and their precedence, with the user scope resolving to ~/.claude/settings.json (lowest precedence) — the only scope this rule inspects.

sources.title: Data usage
sources.notes: States, under Data retention / Local caching: 'Claude Code clients store session transcripts locally in plaintext under ~/.claude/projects/ for 30 days by default to enable session resumption. Adjust the period with cleanupPeriodDays.' Also documents the separate consumer (30-day opt-out / 5-year opt-in) and commercial (30-day standard, Zero Data Retention for qualifying Enterprise configurations) server-side retention terms this rule treats only as a limitation, never an inference.

limitations: Only the user-level ~/.claude/settings.json is inspected; managed, project, local, and CLI-flag scopes are not inspected and can supply or override the effective cleanupPeriodDays value.
An explicit user-level value describes that layer only and does not prove the effective retention window under a managed policy, a specific project, or every invocation.

This rule observes only the local plaintext transcript cache under ~/.claude/projects/; it does not determine Anthropic's separate server-side data-retention period (30 days standard, 5 years for consumer users who opt into training, or Zero Data Retention for qualifying Enterprise configurations), which depends on account type and plan and is never inferred here.

The upper bound of integer_bounds (3650 days) is a conservative operator-chosen cap, not a documented maximum — Anthropic's settings reference documents a minimum of 1 with no stated maximum. A configured value above the cap renders as unrecognized rather than as a numeric finding.

This rule does not evaluate the separate CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_PROMPT_HISTORY environment variable (disables transcript writes entirely) or the --no-session-persistence / persistSession non-interactive and SDK controls, which are documented alongside cleanupPeriodDays but are not settings.json keys.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
cleanupPeriodDays is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers are not inspected.
cleanupPeriodDays set to a value outside the supported integer range (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
